matchup: Phoenix Mercury at Los Angeles Sparks

halftime_model.primary_directional_lean: Los Angeles Sparks team total under 85.5

comparison_summary.spread: Model and market tied at Phoenix -1.5; actual Phoenix margin was 4.
comparison_summary.full_game_total: Market was 0.5 points closer than the model mean, but the model median matched the market and both were directionally correct.
comparison_summary.phoenix_team_total: Model and market were equally accurate at 0.5 points from actual.
comparison_summary.los_angeles_team_total: Model was materially sharper, missing by 2.0 compared with the market's 3.5-point error.
comparison_summary.overall: Near-pin total projection with the clearest model edge appearing on the Sparks team-total under.

confirmed_components: Los Angeles' weak transition production persisted; the Sparks finished with only 3 fast-break points.
Phoenix's interior creation remained the more reliable scoring source, producing 54 paint points on 27-of-36 paint shooting.
Los Angeles' turnover instability persisted and intensified, ending with 18 turnovers that produced 17 Phoenix points.
The game remained close enough to preserve late scoring pressure, but not enough to break the projected total band.
Phoenix sustained offensive efficiency despite poor three-point shooting by shifting toward rim scoring and connective playmaking.
missed_components: The model slightly underestimated Phoenix's closing efficiency, especially a 26-point fourth quarter on 12-of-18 shooting.
The model slightly overestimated Los Angeles' final scoring because fourth-quarter free-throw conversion collapsed to 1-of-3 and half-court turnovers remained costly.
The exact margin was wider than projected because Phoenix won the fourth quarter 26-22 after entering it tied.

third_quarter.note: The game tied 60-60 after three, validating the narrow spread projection and temporarily strengthening overtime risk.

fourth_quarter.note: Phoenix separated through 66.7% shooting, 20 paint points on 10-of-10, and cleaner execution.

decisive_edges: Los Angeles: +11 second-chance points, but this did not offset transition and turnover deficits

Alyssa Thomas.signal: Confirmed as the central live-flow stabilizer and late-game creation hub.

Kahleah Copper.signal: Confirmed Phoenix's best direct scoring outlet despite five fouls.

Natasha Mack.signal: Bench interior production was materially underpriced by the market-facing team-total split.

Rae Burrell.signal: Scoring volume kept Los Angeles competitive, but turnover cost limited team-total upside.

Dearica Hamby.signal: Strong interior production, but Los Angeles lacked enough secondary transition and guard efficiency.

retain: At halftime, team-total decomposition can reveal sharper edges than the full-game total alone.
Transition asymmetry and turnover quality remain essential live indicators.
Interior scoring sustainability should be weighted more heavily than raw three-point regression when a team has an elite facilitator and rim pressure.
add: Create a live Paint Sustainability score using paint attempts, paint efficiency, rim assists, and opponent rim resistance.
Create a Transition Conversion differential using fast-break attempts, points, turnover origin, and live-ball turnover rate.
When model and market full-game totals are within one point, prioritize team-total markets where component projections differ more materially.
Add fourth-quarter foul-state context for primary scorers, but do not automatically downgrade when bench interior replacement is producing efficiently.
Track halftime-to-final team-total error separately from total error to identify which offense the model is mispricing.

retraining_flags: Increase weight on Phoenix-style connective passing and paint conversion when perimeter shooting is weak.
Increase penalty for Sparks-style live-ball turnover exposure and poor transition generation.
Calibrate fourth-quarter scoring distributions for tied games with asymmetric interior efficiency.
Preserve team-total under value when offensive rebounding is strong but conversion efficiency and transition output remain poor.

final_assessment.headline: Near-pin total projection; Sparks team-total under was the sharpest model-over-market edge.

sources: WNBA official scorer's report and play-by-play supplied by user.
